name: Brunnhilde
description: Brunnhilde, also known as Brynhildr, is a prominent figure in Norse mythology, particularly in the Völsunga saga and the Poetic Edda. She is a Valkyrie, a shieldmaiden of Odin, tasked with choosing who lives and dies in battle and escorting the honored dead to Valhalla. Her origins are debated, with some accounts portraying her as a human shieldmaiden elevated to Valkyrie status and others suggesting she was born divine. Brunnhilde possesses immense strength, combat prowess, and strategic intelligence, making her a formidable warrior. She defied Odin's orders by choosing a king he had deemed unworthy to win a battle, leading to her punishment of being imprisoned in a ring of fire, destined to sleep until a hero courageous enough to break through the flames would awaken her. This hero turned out to be Sigurd (or Siegfried in the Nibelungenlied), with whom she fell deeply in love. However, due to a complex web of deceit and betrayal orchestrated by others, Sigurd ultimately married Gudrun, and Brunnhilde, consumed by rage and grief, orchestrated Sigurd's death before taking her own life. Her role is significant, representing themes of fate, love, betrayal, and the complexities of divine justice. She embodies both the power and the vulnerability of the Valkyries, highlighting the conflict between duty and personal desire. Her tale explores the consequences of defying divine authority and the devastating impact of deceit on love and loyalty. Brunnhilde's story is a powerful and tragic one, showcasing the immense strength and ultimate downfall of a legendary Valkyrie.
subtitle: Valkyrie, Warrior Maiden, and Tragic Heroine

keyMyths.title: Brunnhilde's Disobedience
keyMyths.description: Brunnhilde disobeys Odin's command and chooses the 'wrong' king to win a battle. As punishment, Odin imprisons her in a ring of fire.

keyMyths.title: Brunnhilde and Sigurd
keyMyths.description: Sigurd awakens Brunnhilde and they fall in love, but due to a potion and deceit, Sigurd marries Gudrun. This betrayal fuels Brunnhilde's rage.

keyMyths.title: Brunnhilde's Death
keyMyths.description: Consumed by vengeance, Brunnhilde orchestrates Sigurd's death and then chooses to die herself, joining him in Hel or Valhalla.
